title: Add MCP server for ticket/team state management
source: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server patterns for multi-agent state sharing (modelcontextprotocol.io, 2025-2026)
description: Currently, Morty agents have no way to query ticket state, read team messages, or update their status during execution — they receive a static prompt and return text. By exposing CTO state as an MCP server, agents can dynamically read/write tickets, send team messages, check file reservations, and query ADRs during execution. This transforms the one-shot delegation model into a truly interactive multi-agent system.
proposed_changes: Create `scripts/mcp_server.py` implementing an MCP server (using the `mcp` Python SDK) with these tools: (1) `get_ticket(ticket_id)` — read ticket state and dependencies; (2) `update_ticket_status(ticket_id, status, output)` — partial progress updates; (3) `send_team_message(team_id, to, message)` — real-time inter-agent messaging; (4) `get_team_context(team_id)` — read shared decisions/interfaces; (5) `list_files_reserved(team_id)` — check file ownership; (6) `read_adr(name)` — access architecture decisions. In delegate.py, modify `delegate_to_agent()` to pass `--mcp-server scripts/mcp_server.py` when invoking `claude -p`, giving each Morty access to these tools. Update agent JSON configs to document available MCP tools in their identity/constraints.
retry_note: Retry of failed evolve. Original timeout 300s. Bumped impact 9->10 to ensure top priority.
